channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Telstra Outage Highlights Ongoing Vulnerabilities in Critical Infrastructure
item.description: The recent Telstra outage has reignited concerns about the reliability of vital infrastructure in Australia, just 10 months after the Optus disaster that disrupted emergency services. The incident, wh...

item.title: US and Iran Escalate Conflict with New Strikes on Hormuz Strait
item.description: The ongoing conflict between the United States and Iran has intensified with renewed military strikes targeting each other's assets in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. US forces launched airs...

item.title: Hayley Wickenheiser leaves Toronto Maple Leafs after eight seasons
item.description: Hayley Wickenheiser, a hockey legend with a storied playing career, has parted ways with the Toronto Maple Leafs after eight seasons in the team's front office. The decision came after discussions wit...

item.title: ITV relocates World Cup studio indoors due to severe weather disruption in New York
item.description: ITV's World Cup coverage team was forced to abandon their New York studio during France's quarter-final clash with Morocco due to severe storms. The studio, located in Brooklyn, was evacuated as heavy...

item.title: Philippines Reaffirms Sovereignty Over Batanes Amid China's Academic Claims
item.description: The Philippine Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) has firmly rejected claims by supposed Chinese scholars that the Batan Islands belong to Taiwan, which China asserts as part of its territory. The DF...

item.title: Toronto corrections officer faces legal challenges over alleged misconduct and security threats
item.description: A senior corrections officer at Toronto South Detention Centre is facing legal scrutiny over allegations of abusive practices, including improper strip searches and threatening inmates, which are cent...

item.title: Boat Fire on Lake Victoria Claims Two Lives, Leaves Others Missing
item.description: A passenger and cargo boat caught fire on Lake Victoria while traveling from Gozba Island in Kagera Region to Mwanza, resulting in two deaths and leaving 50 others missing. The incident occurred betwe...

item.title: TikTok Dancer Sycross Thanks Musician Lasmid for Prosthetic Leg Support
item.description: TikTok dancer Sycross, known as the Disableboy, has publicly thanked Ghanaian musician Lasmid after the singer fulfilled his promise to help him obtain a prosthetic leg. Sycross, who has gained a larg...

item.title: Senate Approves Lafarge Acquisition by Chinese Investor, Guarantees Nigerian Equity Protection
item.description: The Nigerian Senate has approved the proposed acquisition of Lafarge Africa Plc by Hainan Huaxin Pan-African Investment Company, assuring that local investors' 16.19% equity in the cement company rema...

item.title: Foreign firms compete in Africa’s digital identity market
item.description: The national identity card in Africa has evolved beyond a simple administrative tool to become a critical access key for public and private services. This shift has sparked a competitive market worth ...

item.title: France and Morocco Clash in World Cup Quarter-Final as Mbappe's Penalty Saved
item.description: France face their most significant challenge yet at the World Cup as they take on Morocco in a repeat of the 2022 semi-final. Didier Deschamps' squad, led by Kylian Mbappe, has dominated the tournamen...

item.title: Akira Kurosawa's 'Ran' explores family succession and chaos in feudal Japan
item.description: The article discusses Akira Kurosawa's 1985 film 'Ran', a Shakespearean adaptation set in 16th-century Japan. It delves into the story of a noble family's succession crisis, which spirals into violenc...

item.title: Key Philippine and Global Headlines for July 9, 2026
item.description: The article highlights the most significant news stories from July 9, 2026. Key points include Vice President Sara Duterte's chief of staff, Zuleika Lopez, making a surprise appearance at the Senate, ...

item.title: Anthony Albanese's apology highlights media fragmentation amid political podcast culture
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese faced backlash after making an inappropriate joke about Kylie Minogue during a podcast game, sparking discussions about the challenges of maintaining decorum in public ...

item.title: Pastor’s Wife Faces Trial Delay After Co-Accused Pleads Guilty in R4.1m Fraud Case
item.description: Lingasperi 'Leigh' Madurai, a former human resources manager at P&A Fabricators, faces fraud charges after her co-accused, former creditors clerk Senamile Mthabela, pleaded guilty to receiving R257,32...

item.title: IGP Calls for Global Tech Collaboration in Policing While Stressing Importance of Public Trust
item.description: Inspector General of Police, Tunji Disu, has urged global cooperation to enhance security through technological advancements while emphasizing that public trust remains irreplaceable. During the Fifth...

item.title: Bwala's comparison of £2,600 UK income with ₦60,000 salary in Nigeria sparks widespread criticism online
item.description: Daniel Bwala, Special Adviser to President Bola Tinubu on Policy Communication, has generated serious public debate after saying that, in some situations, a Nigerian earning ₦60,000 every month may be...

item.title: France and Morocco clash in FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final
item.description: France and Morocco commenced their FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final clash in Boston, with both teams showcasing tactical discipline and resilience. The match, which began at 6:03am, saw early chances...

item.title: ABS-CBN Studios Launches First Vertical Drama 'The Chambermaid’s Daughter' on iWant
item.description: ABS-CBN Studios has officially launched its first vertical drama, 'The Chambermaid’s Daughter,' on the iWant platform, marking a significant step in the evolution of digital content delivery. This dra...

item.title: Economist Suggests Reforming Equalization Payments to Reduce Provincial Tensions
item.description: Trevor Tombe, an economist at the University of Calgary, argues that Canada's current equalization payment system, which bases transfers on provincial revenue generation, fuels resentment between weal...
